concepts.title: Accounting for Not-for-Profit Organisations is a separate schedule in the Companies Act, 2013
concepts.description: The accounting for not-for-profit organisations is governed by a separate schedule in the Companies Act, 2013, making it a key area of focus for the board.

concepts.title: The accounting treatment for not-for-profit organisations is similar to that of partnership firms
concepts.description: Both not-for-profit organisations and partnership firms follow a similar accounting treatment, making it essential to understand the similarities and differences between the two.

concepts.title: Not-for-profit organisations do not distribute surplus among members
concepts.description: One of the key characteristics of not-for-profit organisations is that they do not distribute surplus among their members, unlike profit-making entities.

concepts.title: Revenue Recognition: Accrual Method, Matching Concept
concepts.description: Not-for-profit organisations follow the accrual method of accounting and the matching concept for revenue recognition, which is crucial for accurate financial reporting.

concepts.title: Confusing Not-for-Profit Organisations with Partnership Firms
concepts.description: Students often confuse not-for-profit organisations with partnership firms, but it's essential to understand the differences in their accounting treatment and characteristics.

concepts.title: NFP = Not For Profit, Not for Distribution
concepts.description: The mnemonic 'NFP = Not For Profit, Not for Distribution' helps students remember the key characteristics of not-for-profit organisations.

concepts.title: Accounting Standards for Not-for-Profit Organisations are prescribed by the Accounting Standards Board (ASB)
concepts.description: The Accounting Standards Board (ASB) prescribes the accounting standards for not-for-profit organisations, making it a key area of focus for the board.

concepts.title: The financial statements of not-for-profit organisations are presented in a similar format to those of partnership firms
concepts.description: The financial statements of not-for-profit organisations are presented in a similar format to those of partnership firms, with some exceptions.
